name: Yes4All Adjustable Kettlebell 12–42 lbs
whatWorks: Six weight settings from 12 to 42 lbs replace six separate kettlebells — the space saving in a home gym is significant, and the cost savings over buying 6 individual bells is even more so ($300–500+ depending on weights).
The vinyl coating protects wood and tile floors from the cast iron base, which matters in homes where kettlebell training happens on hardwood or laminate surfaces.
Quick-change dial adjustment takes about 15 seconds between weights, which is fast enough for circuit training where you're switching between exercises and weights without resting long.
The weight range (12–42 lbs) is practical for the full range of typical kettlebell movements — goblet squats and swings at higher weights, Turkish get-ups and presses at lower weights.
worthKnowing: The handle diameter (~35mm) is slightly larger than traditional solid cast kettlebells (~33mm). This is noticeable on exercises with a two-handed grip — cleans and snatches in particular — and some lifters find it reduces comfort on high-rep sets.
The adjustment mechanism can develop minor looseness over time with heavy use, resulting in a slight rattle during dynamic movements. Not a safety issue, but noticeable.
Not suitable for kettlebell sport or competitive use — the non-standard handle and adjustment mechanism make it unsuitable for the precise grip positions used in GS-style lifts.
The vinyl coating can tear at the seam around the adjustment plate after extended use. Underlying cast iron remains functional, but the coating damage is cosmetically obvious.

commonComplaints.title: Handle slightly thicker than real kettlebells…
commonComplaints.body: The handle is noticeably thicker than my other kettlebells. Fine for swings and squats but for cleans and snatches it changes the feel in a way I don't like.

commonComplaints.title: Adjustment dial loosens over time…
commonComplaints.body: After about 3 months of daily use the dial got a bit rattly. Nothing dangerous, but I can hear it on every swing now. Still works fine.

commonComplaints.title: Vinyl coating tearing at seam…
commonComplaints.body: The vinyl started peeling at the adjustment seam after about 6 months. The cast iron underneath is fine, it just looks bad. Wish they used a more durable coating.

commonComplaints.title: Not for competitive kettlebell use…
commonComplaints.body: If you train GS-style this is not the right tool. The handle geometry is wrong and the adjustment mechanism changes the balance. Get a competition bell instead.

verdict: The Yes4All Adjustable Kettlebell earns its place in home gyms by solving the space and cost problem of owning multiple kettlebells. Six weights in one footprint, fast adjustment, and floor-safe vinyl coating make it practical for general kettlebell training — swings, goblet squats, presses, and carries. The handle thickness is the honest caveat for experienced kettlebell lifters who will notice the difference from traditional bells. For the large majority of home gym users who want kettlebell training without buying multiple separate bells, this delivers excellent value.

realBuyers.quote: This replaced 5 kettlebells that were taking up a third of my garage. Worth every dollar for the space alone.
I do circuits switching between 22 and 32 lbs constantly. The dial change takes about 10 seconds — totally fine for training.
Cost me $130 instead of $350+ for the equivalent individual bells. No-brainer.
Perfect for KB swings, goblet squats, and presses at home. Exactly what I needed.

sources.title: Best Adjustable Kettlebells 2025 — Garage Gym Reviews

metaDescription: Yes4All Adjustable Kettlebell review for Canada: 12–42 lbs in 6 settings, vinyl-coated cast iron, quick-change dial. Honest on handle thickness and durability. ~$129 CAD.
introText: The Yes4All Adjustable Kettlebell replaces six separate bells in one compact unit — 12 to 42 lbs in six settings with a quick-change dial. It's the practical choice for home gym users who want kettlebell training without the footprint or cost of a full rack of bells. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07RXBT1RH) at around $129 CAD.
